question: Cyclohexylamine when treated with nitrous acid yields (P). On treating (P) with PCC results in (Q). When (Q) is heated with dil. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>NaOH</mi></math> we get (R) The final product (R) is:
solution: Cyclohexylamine when treated with nitrous acid yields cyclohexanol.On treating cyclohexanol with PCC which is a mild oxidising agent and oxidises the alcohols into aldehyde (in case of primary alcohol) or ketones (in case of secondary alcohol) in the presence of dichloromethane results cyclohexanone. When cyclohexanone is heated with dilute NaOH dehydration of aldol takes place by the removal of water molecules we get the final product.

question:

A. Chloroxylenol
B. Bithional
C. Veronal
D. Prontosil
E. Terpineol

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
solution:
Antiseptics and disinfectants are also chemicals that either kill or prevent the growth of microorganisms. Antiseptics are applied to living tissues such as wounds, cuts, ulcers and diseased skin surfaces. Examples are furacine, soframicine, etc. Commonly used antiseptic, Dettol is a mixture of chloroxylenol and terpineol. Bithionol (the compound is also called bithional) is added to soaps to impart antiseptic properties. Protonsil is an antibiotic, whereas veronal is a tranquilizer which is used for the treatment of stress and mild or severe mental diseases.

So, Veronal and Prontosil are not used as disinfectant.

Reason (R): In 3d series of elements successive filling of d-orbitals takes place.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below:
question:
Given below are two statements: one is labelled as Assertion (A) and the other is labelled as Reason (R).

Assertion (A): The first ionization enthalpy of 3d series elements is more than that of group 2 metals

Reason (R): In 3d series of elements successive filling of d-orbitals takes place.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below:
options: Both (A) and (R) are true and (R) is the correct explanation of (A)
Both (A) and (R) are true but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A)
solution:
Metal Sc Ti V Cr Mn Fe Co Ni Cu Zn
IE (kJ/mol) 631 656 650 653 717 762 758 736 745 906
 
Metal Be Mg Ca Sr Ba Ra
IE (kJ/mol) 899 737 590 549 503 509

In the first transition series, the first and second ionization enthalpy values increase slowly on moving across the period. As the nuclear charge rises, the interaction between the nucleus and valence electrons increases, and as a result, the ionization enthalpy increases. So, Sc has the lowest value of ionization enthalpy while Zn has the highest value of ionization enthalpy.

So, both assertion and reasons are true and reason is the correct expalantion of assertion.

q:
If the CFSE of TiH2O63+ is -96.0 kJ/mol, this complex will absorb maximum at wavelength nm. (nearest integer)

Assume Planck' constant (h)=6.4×10-34Js, Speed of light (c)=3.0×108 m/s and Avogadro's constant NA=6×1023/mol.
question:
If the CFSE of TiH2O63+ is -96.0 kJ/mol, this complex will absorb maximum at wavelength nm. (nearest integer)

Assume Planck' constant (h)=6.4×10-34Js, Speed of light (c)=3.0×108 m/s and Avogadro's constant NA=6×1023/mol.
solution:
In TiH2O63+, oxidation state of Titanium is +3.
Thus, electronic configuration:

Ti+3:3d1⇒t2g1,0,0,eg0,0

We know CFSE =-0.4nt2g+0.6negΔo       (      Δo = Octahedral splitting energy)

Δo=96×1030.4×6×1023=4×10-19 J

Using planck's equation and fact that octahedral splitting energy per complex is:
⇒Δo=hcλ=4×10-19 J

⇒λ=6.4×10-34×3×1084×10-19=4.8×10-7 m

⇒λ=480×10-9 m

∴λ=480 nm

q: In Dumas method for the estimation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>, the sample is heated with copper oxide and the gas evolved is passed over:
question: In Dumas method for the estimation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></math>, the sample is heated with copper oxide and the gas evolved is passed over:
solution:
In Dumas method, the nitrogen-containing organic compound, when heated with copper oxide in an atmosphere of carbon dioxide, yields free nitrogen in addition to carbon dioxide and water.

Traces of nitrogen oxides formed, if any, are reduced to nitrogen by passing the gaseous mixture over a heated copper gauze. The mixture of gases so produced is collected over an aqueous solution of potassium hydroxide which absorbs carbon dioxide

So, the correct answer is Copper gauze.

q: The Lewis acid character of boron tri halides follows the order:
question: The Lewis acid character of boron tri halides follows the order:
solution:
The strength of the acidic character of boron trihalides depends upon π-π back bonding. In boron trihalides, π-π -back bonding occurs due to empty orbital of boron and filled orbitals of halogens.

 

The π-π back bonding is shown maximum byBF3, as the size of B and F are small and comparatively same. Due to this effect tendency of accepting lone-pair of electrons of boron decreases as the size of halogen increases. The order of size of halogens are F < Cl < Br < I . Thus, acidic nature is in order BF3<BCl3<BBr3<Bl3.

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
question:
Evaluate the following statements for their correctness.

A. The elevation in boiling point temperature of water will be same for 0.1 M NaCl and 0.1 M urea.
B. Azeotropic mixture boil without change in their composition.
C. Osmosis always takes place from hypertonic to hypotonic solution.
D. The density of 32% H2SO4 solution having molarity 4.09 M is approximately 1.26 g mL–1.
E. A negatively charged sol is obtained when KI solution is added to silver nitrate solution.

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
solution:
(A) Elevation in boiling point (∆Tb) temperature of the water is not same, as ‘i’ value is different for two solutions.
(B) Azeotropic mixture: Liquid mixtures that distill over without changes in composition are called constant boiling mixtures or Azeotropes or Azeotropic mixtures.
(C) Osmosis always takes place from hypotonic (low concentration of solute) solution to hypertonic (high concentration of solute) solution.
(D) Let the mass of H2SO4 (32%) is 100.

∴ Weight of H2SO4 = 32

Moles of H2SO4 = 3298

Now, 4.09 = 3298 × V

⇒ V = 79 mL

Density = 10079 = 1.265
(E) A negative charged sol is obtained when KI solution is added to silver nitrate solution whereas positive charged sol is obtained when AgNO3 solution is added to KI solution.

So, the correct answer is B and D only.

q:
Arrange the following orbitals in decreasing order of energy.

A. n=3, l=0, m=0
B. n=4, l=0, m=0
C. n=3, l=1, m=0
D. n=3, l=2, m=1

The correct option for the order is:
question:
Arrange the following orbitals in decreasing order of energy.

A. n=3, l=0, m=0
B. n=4, l=0, m=0
C. n=3, l=1, m=0
D. n=3, l=2, m=1

The correct option for the order is:
solution:
In multi-electronic species, energy is decided on the basis of (n+l) rule.

3s = 3 + 0 + 0= 3

4s = 4 + 0 +0 = 4

3p = 3 + 1 +0 = 4

3d = 3 + 2 + 1 = 6

For 4s and 3p, an orbital with a higher value of n has higher energy.

So increasing the order of energy is 3d>4s>3p>3s.

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
solution:
(A) In physisorption, the enthalpy heat is low and lies between 20 - 40 kJ mol-1. In physisorption, multilayers of adsorbed particles are formed.

(B) In chemisorption, the enthalpy heat is high and lies within the range of 40 - 200 kJ mol-1.  In chemisorption, a monolayer of adsorbed particles is formed.

(C) N2g+3H2g⟶Fe(s)2NH3g

Heterogeneous catalysts occupy a different phase. Generally, heterogeneous catalysts are solid compounds that are added to liquid or gas reaction mixtures. Fe is used as a heterogeneous catalyst in the Haber process.

(D) In the process of adsorption chromatography, different compounds are adsorbed on the adsorbent to different degrees based on the absorptivity of the component. 

So, the correct matching is A-IV, B-II, C-III, D-I.

solution:
Given condition is, p∧q→r∨q∧p∧r→q).....(1)

We know conditional disjunction as,

p→q≡~p∨q

Hence, p∧r→q≡~p∧r∨q

Using De Morgan's law

p∧r→q≡~p∧r∨q≡~p∨~r∨q

Equation 1 can be written as,

⇒p∧q→r∨q∧~p∨~r∨q)

⇒~p∧q∨r∨q∧~p∨~r∨q)

⇒~p∨~q∨r∨q∧~p∨~r∨q)

⇒(~p∨~q∨r∨q∧~p∨~r∨q)

⇒~p∨t∧~p∨~r∨q)

⇒~p∨~r∨q

For above statement to be a tautology,

r can be ~p or q

When r=~p or r=q⇒~p∨~r∨q=t

∴ Two value of r are possible

question:

solution:
Given,

a, b ∈ I and relation T is defined as a2-b2∈I

Now if a2-b2∈I so b2-a2 will also be integer, for example if 52-42=9 is integer then 42-52=-9 is also a integer,

Now checking transitive,

If 42-32=7 is an integer, 32-22=5 is an integer then 42-22=12 is also an integer, hence we can say that T is symmetric and transitive,

 Now checking relation S which is defined as 2+ab>0,

So, if we replace ab by -19 then 2+ab>0 is true but we take ba=-9 for symmetric we get 2+ba=2-9=-7≯0 hence, the relation is not symmetric, 

Now checking transitive, now if 2+ab>0⇒ab>-2 and bc>-2 then we cannot say that ac>-2,

For example if we take 41>-2, 1-1>-2 then 4-1≯-2, hence it is not transitive,

Hence, we can say that  T is symmetric and S is not.

⇒3c→×2b→×a→=0

9c→2+4b→2+12b→·c→=λ2a→2

⇒36+1+12×12×2cos2π3=λ231

⇒λ2=1

Now putting the value of λ in 3c→×2b→=λa→ we get,

3c→+2b→=±a→      …1

Now taking dot product with b→ in above equation we get,

3b→·c→+2b→·b→=±a→·b→

⇒c→×a→2=49a→×b→2

q:
Two parallel plate capacitors C1 and C2 each having capacitance of 10 μF are individually charged by a 100 V D.C. source. Capacitor C1 is kept connected to the source and a dielectric slab is inserted between it plates. Capacitor C2 is disconnected from the source and then a dielectric slab is inserted in it. Afterwards the capacitor C1 is also disconnected from the source and the two capacitors are finally connected in parallel combination. The common potential of the combination will be _____ V.

(Assuming Dielectric constant =10)
question:
Two parallel plate capacitors C1 and C2 each having capacitance of 10 μF are individually charged by a 100 V D.C. source. Capacitor C1 is kept connected to the source and a dielectric slab is inserted between it plates. Capacitor C2 is disconnected from the source and then a dielectric slab is inserted in it. Afterwards the capacitor C1 is also disconnected from the source and the two capacitors are finally connected in parallel combination. The common potential of the combination will be _____ V.

(Assuming Dielectric constant =10)
solution:
As source is connected in the process therefore, additional charge will be provided. Therefore, charge on C1=KCV.

Since source is disconnected, so its charge will remain the same. Then, charge on C2=CV.

New capacitance in both cases will become, KC.

When they are connected in parallel charge will be equally divided so charge on one capacitor is q=K+12CV.

So common potential,

V'=qKC=K+12KV=55 V.

q: Considering a group of positive charges, which of the following statements is correct?
question: Considering a group of positive charges, which of the following statements is correct?
options: Net potential of the system cannot be zero at a point but net electric field can be zero at that point
Net potential of the system at a point can be zero but net electric field can't be zero at that point
Both the net potential and the net field can be zero at a point
Both the net potential and the net electric field cannot be zero at a point
solution:
As we know, net potential will be algebraic sum of potential due to each charge, so we can write Vnet=∑kQiri.

As given in the question, Qi and ri are positive, so

∴  Vnet>0

But electric field is a vector quantity, so it can be zero for a given arrangement of charges. For example, electric field at the centre of the two equal charges at certain distance will be zero as electric field due to both charges will be in opposite direction and hence will cancel each other.

solution:
Final distance from the centre of earth wil be R+9R=10R.

Now acceleration due to gravity at any height(distance from centre r) is given by,

g=GMr2.

Therefore,

g'=GM10R2

Now, value of g at surface will be, GMR2.

Hence,

g'=g100.

Then, W'=mg'=mg100=W100

question: A body of mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi>kg</mi></math> is moving with an initial speed of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. The body stops after <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math> due to friction between body and the floor. The value of the coefficient of friction is: (Take acceleration due to gravity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>g</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math>)
solution:
Friction force wil act opposite to the motion of the body.

F=μN=μmg

⇒a=-μg-ve sign for direction relative to the motion of body

Now, applying equation of motion, we get

v=u+at

⇒0=20+-μ×10×5

⇒50μ=20

 ⇒μ=0.4

question: A long conducting wire having a current <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>I</mi></math> flowing through it, is bent into a circular coil of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>N</mi></math> turns. Then it is bent into a circular coil of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi></math> turns. The magnetic field is calculated at the centre of coils in both the cases. The ratio of the magnetic field in first case to that of second case is:
solution:
As in both cases, length of the wire will remain the same, therefore

l=2πRN=2πrn⇒Rr=nN

Now magnetic field due to single circular coil is given by, B=μ0I2r.

Therefore, required ratio of total magnetic field wil be,

B1B2=Nμ0I2Rnμ0I2r=N2n2
